title: Foundation Checkpoint: Defend the Evidence
summary: Complete a broad selected-response and constructed-response checkpoint with misconception-specific correction across every foundation competency.
objectives: Demonstrate stable retrieval of all six models under reduced support.
Explain why a tempting incorrect account conflicts with available evidence.

steps.title: Interpret the trace · defense checkpoint
steps.instruction: In the foundation defense checkpoint, what does the read event establish?
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Working through interpret the trace narrows the foundation defense checkpoint before the result is shown.

content.title: Interpret the trace — foundation defense checkpoint
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the foundation defense checkpoint evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During foundation defense checkpoint predict, identify which system chain boundary “Interpret the trace” asks about.
Compare every foundation defense checkpoint option with the named “Interpret the trace” evidence during predict.
For foundation defense checkpoint predict, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Interpret the trace.”

options.text: The browser permanently rewrote the file as soon as the pointer was clicked.
The operating system supplied stored document bytes before browser parsing began.
The display requested HTML tags directly from storage.

steps.title: Choose the next evidence · defense checkpoint
steps.instruction: In the foundation defense checkpoint, the local file works, but the HTTPS URL returns 404. What should be inspected first?
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Working through choose the next evidence narrows the foundation defense checkpoint while you defend the selected claim.

content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the foundation defense checkpoint evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

options.text: The local monitor cable, because the file rendered successfully.
The requested URL path and the server response evidence.
The HTML heading color, because status codes are produced by CSS.

steps.title: Separate source and DOM · defense checkpoint
steps.instruction: In the foundation defense checkpoint, which statement accounts for the recovered tree?
steps.why: Source, the browser-built DOM, layout, and pixels are related stages rather than interchangeable evidence. Working through separate source and dom narrows the foundation defense checkpoint as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A browser parses source into object models, applies error-recovery rules, derives accessibility information and styles, computes layout, and paints output. Source shows the input text; the DOM shows the current parsed structure; computed styles and layout show resolved presentation; pixels show only the final visual result. Apply this distinction during the foundation defense checkpoint read task.

hints: During foundation defense checkpoint read, identify which browser pipeline boundary “Separate source and DOM” asks about.
Compare every foundation defense checkpoint option with the named “Separate source and DOM” evidence during read.
For foundation defense checkpoint read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Separate source and DOM.”

options.text: Source and DOM must remain identical character for character after parsing.
Source is parser input; the DOM is the browser’s current constructed representation and may include recovery.
The DOM is a screenshot made from the final pixels.

steps.title: Rebuild devtools evidence · defense checkpoint
steps.instruction: Within the foundation defense checkpoint, reconstruct the devtools evidence in causal order.
steps.why: A deliberate panel choice turns browser inspection into evidence instead of random experimentation. Ordering the foundation defense checkpoint exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the foundation defense checkpoint.

hints: In the foundation defense checkpoint sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate foundation defense checkpoint devtools evidence events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the foundation defense checkpoint sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Reproduce the defect and state the expected behavior
Choose the panel that exposes the responsible layer
Run the smallest temporary experiment that tests the hypothesis
Repair saved source, reload, and verify the evidence again

steps.title: Write the evidence note · defense checkpoint
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the foundation defense checkpoint. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the foundation defense checkpoint from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the foundation defense checkpoint case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these foundation defense checkpoint technical anchors where they fit: operating system, browser, display, folder, relative path.
Close the foundation defense checkpoint note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.

options.text: The monitor cached the old pixels inside the display cable.
The Elements edit changed the current DOM experiment, not the saved HTML file.
HTML headings are forbidden from being saved after DevTools opens.

steps.title: Transfer Use a reduced test · defense checkpoint
steps.instruction: In the foundation defense checkpoint transfer round, what does a minimal browser case contribute?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through use a reduced test narrows the foundation defense checkpoint transfer round as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A useful technical question names the exact behavior and environment. Primary specifications define the platform; official documentation explains it; compatibility data narrows support; and a reduced browser test verifies the local claim. Search rank, confident tone, and copied snippets are not authority. Apply this distinction during the foundation defense checkpoint transfer round read task.

hints: During foundation defense checkpoint transfer round read, identify which source verification boundary “Use a reduced test” asks about.
Compare every foundation defense checkpoint transfer round option with the named “Use a reduced test” evidence during read.
For foundation defense checkpoint transfer round read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Use a reduced test.”

options.text: It replaces the need to identify the tested browser and version.
It connects the documented claim to observable behavior while removing unrelated project variables.
It proves every browser and assistive technology behaves identically.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the foundation defense checkpoint transfer round.

hints: In the foundation defense checkpoint transfer round sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate foundation defense checkpoint transfer round system chain events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the foundation defense checkpoint transfer round sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Input hardware reports the open-file action
The operating system reads document bytes from storage
The browser parses the received bytes and prepares output
Graphics hardware and the display produce visible pixels

options.text: The web is the physical cables, while the internet is only the pages displayed in browsers.
The internet supplies network infrastructure; the web uses it to exchange linked resources through web protocols.
Internet and web are exact synonyms with no useful distinction.
